qa_pairs.question: How can one limit the maximum size of each slice when creating a `dar` archive?
qa_pairs.answer: To limit the maximum size of each slice in bytes when creating a `dar` archive, use the flag `-s` followed by a number and one of the units: k (kilobytes), M (megabytes), G (gigabytes), or T (terabytes). For example, `dar -s 100M -w -c monday -g test` would create slices that are a maximum of 100 MB each.

qa_pairs.question: If a `dar` archive is split into multiple slices, how does one extract all of them using the `dar` utility?
qa_pairs.answer: When a `dar` archive is split into multiple slices (e.g., `monday.1.dar`, `monday.2.dar`), you can extract all of them by using only the base name of the archive with the extraction command. For example, `dar -O -x monday` would extract all slices associated with the `monday` base name.
